growth_records.snippet: Synechococcus WH8102 ... SN ... 23 ... 45 (14:10) ... Exponential phase growth rate (d-1) 0.54
growth_records.notes: Table 1 reports exponential-phase growth rate = 0.54 d-1 directly (RFU-based, batch tube culture, 14:10 h light:dark, 45 umol photons m-2 s-1). Converted to per-hour: mu = 0.54/24 = 0.0225 h-1; doubling_time_h = ln2/0.0225 = 30.81 h.

growth_records.snippet: No significant difference in growth rate or maximum cell densities was observed for Synechococcus sp. WH7803 when grown in the presence/absence of the heterotroph during exponential phase (38.2 and 38.7 h doubling times,
growth_records.notes: Axenic culture doubling time of 38.7 h explicitly reported for exponential phase growth in ASW (no heterotroph present). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/38.7 = 0.0179 h^-1. Cultured in 100 ml ASW in 250 ml Erlenmeyer flasks at 22°C, 10 μmol photons m-2 s-1 light, 140 rpm shaking (Methods). ASW contained no vitamins; N and P supplied as stated unless noted otherwise.

growth_records.snippet: Synechococcus is expected to have faster growth rates at these temperatures, doubling every 1.1 ± 0.2 d (refs. 12,46).
growth_records.notes: This is a literature-derived assumption cited from refs. 12 and 46 (not a direct measurement by the present field study) used to model virus-induced mortality in the North Pacific transition zone ('these temperatures' = low water-column temperatures of the study region; exact °C not given). doubling_time_h = 1.1 d x 24 = 26.4 h; growth_rate_per_h = ln2/26.4 = 0.0263 h^-1. No defined culture medium (in situ marine population). [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, medium]

growth_records.medium: Artificial seawater (defined; NO3-:PO4^3- input ratio = 1.7, i.e. N-stress; full composition in Table S1)
growth_records.cite: Garcia et al. Proteome trait regulation of marine Synechococcus elemental stoichiometry under global change. ISME J (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae046. [PMC11020310]
growth_records.snippet: We used two concentration ratios of macronutrients (NO3-:PO43- = 1.7 and 80) and three levels of temperature (20, 24, and 28C)... We measured our fixed dilution rate across treatments at 0.178 +/- 0.004 (mean +/- SD) day-1
growth_records.notes: Chemostat at steady state so mu = D. Reported D = 0.178 day-1. mu(h-1) = 0.178/24 = 0.007417 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.178 day = 3.894 day = 93.46 h. Condition: N-stress (NO3:PO4 input = 1.7) at 20C. Full artificial-seawater medium composition is in supplementary Table S1, not present in the fulltext XML, hence composition:[] and stated:false. No specific substrate uptake or product secretion fluxes (mmol/gDW/h) reported in fulltext; nutrient uptake could in principle be derived as q = D x cellular quota but numeric C/N/P quotas are given only in figures, not in extractable text, so not computed to avoid fabrication.

growth_records.snippet: Our experimental design covered P-stress (N:P_input = 80:1) and N-stress (N:P_input = 1.7:1) at 20C, 24C, and 28C. We measured our fixed dilution rate across treatments at 0.178 +/- 0.004 (mean +/- SD) day-1
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state so mu = D. Fixed D = 0.178 day-1. mu(h-1) = 0.178/24 = 0.007417 h-1. td = ln2/0.178 day = 3.894 day = 93.46 h. Condition: P-stress (NO3:PO4 input = 80) at 20C. Full medium composition only in Table S1, absent from fulltext -> composition:[]/stated:false. No flux rates in extractable text.

growth_records.medium: f/2 medium (Guillard and Ryther 1962) in filtered seawater, augmented with 12C/13C-bicarbonate; SIP calibration treatment f_media=0.32
growth_records.cite: Gordon T. Taylor et al. Single-Cell Growth Rates in Photoautotrophic Populations Measured by Stable Isotope Probing and Resonance Raman Microspectrometry. Frontiers in Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2017.01449. [PMC5541042]
growth_records.snippet: median growth rates computed over the entire exponential phase of f media 0.32 and 0.43 cultures were 0.22 +/- 0.07 and 0.20 +/- 0.07 day-1 for single cells (Raman-derived) and 0.22 +/- 0.07 and 0.22 +/- 0.07 day-1 for populations (fluoresc
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote = cyanobacterium Synechococcus sp. RS9916. f_media=0.32 treatment. Reported median growth rates over exponential phase (0-18 d): population (in vivo chlorophyll-a fluorescence, ln AFU vs time slope) = 0.22 +/- 0.07 day-1; single-cell (SCRR Raman) = 0.22 +/- 0.07 day-1. growth_rate_per_h converted from 0.22 day-1: 0.22/24 = 0.00917 h-1. doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.22 = 3.15 day = 75.6 h (paper states g ~ 3.3 days for these slow-growing photoautotrophs). Paper also reports overall mean mu_pop ~0.21 day-1 from 0-18 d for both 0.32 and 0.43 populations. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates reported (photoautotroph fixing dissolved inorganic 13C-bicarbonate; not quantified as mmol/gDW/h). This is a SIP/Raman single-cell method paper, not a 13C-MFA flux-partitioning study, so no mfa_fluxes.

growth_records.medium: f/2 medium (Guillard and Ryther 1962) in filtered seawater, augmented with 12C/13C-bicarbonate; SIP calibration treatment f_media=0.43
growth_records.cite: Gordon T. Taylor et al. Single-Cell Growth Rates in Photoautotrophic Populations Measured by Stable Isotope Probing and Resonance Raman Microspectrometry. Frontiers in Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2017.01449. [PMC5541042]
growth_records.snippet: median growth rates computed over the entire exponential phase of f media 0.32 and 0.43 cultures were 0.22 +/- 0.07 and 0.20 +/- 0.07 day-1 for single cells (Raman-derived) and 0.22 +/- 0.07 and 0.22 +/- 0.07 day-1 for populations (fluoresc
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote = cyanobacterium Synechococcus sp. RS9916. f_media=0.43 treatment. Reported median growth rates over exponential phase (0-18 d): population (in vivo chlorophyll-a fluorescence) = 0.22 +/- 0.07 day-1; single-cell (SCRR Raman) = 0.20 +/- 0.07 day-1. growth_rate_per_h converted from population 0.22 day-1: 0.22/24 = 0.00917 h-1 (single-cell 0.20 day-1 = 0.20/24 = 0.00833 h-1). doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.22 = 3.15 day = 75.6 h (paper states g ~ 3.3 days). Growth conditions deliberately held constant across f_media treatments (C_T fixed while f_media manipulated) so growth is essentially identical to the 0.32 record. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates reported (photoautotroph). Not a 13C-MFA flux study; no mfa_fluxes.

growth_records.medium: modified SN medium (75% filtered seawater + 25% milli-Q), N:P input = 441
growth_records.cite: Kretz, Cecilia B. et al. Influence of growth rate on the physiological response of marine Synechococcus to phosphate limitation. Frontiers in Microbiology (2015). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2015.00085. [PMC4324148]
growth_records.snippet: growth rate was manipulated by changing the speed of the pump ... which in turn changed the dilution rate. We changed the dilution rate 3 times and the resulting growth rate averaged 0.7 day-1, 0.3 day-1, and 0.2 day-1 per strain.
growth_records.notes: Marine cyanobacterium (prokaryote), P-limited chemostat. At steady state mu=D. Highest dilution: D=0.7 day-1 => mu=0.7/24=0.02917 h-1 (calculated). td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.7=0.990 day=23.8 h (calculated). Photoautotroph: inorganic C source (95 uM Na2CO3 / dissolved CO2), no organic substrate uptake; phosphate is the limiting nutrient (residual SRP 1.0-9.2 nM measured) but no gDW-specific uptake rate (mmol/gDW/h) reported, so uptake_rates empty. Ks,p for WH8102 = 0.8-2.5 nM; drawdown c = 0.9-1.2 nM. Cellular C:N:P (rep1 vessel) 187:31:1. pH not reported.

growth_records.snippet: Rapid specific growth rates (mu) of up to 0.2 h-1 (doubling time of 3.5 h) were achieved when Synechococcus 7002 was cultivated in turbidostat mode under light-limited conditions at 30C ... approached a maximum of 0.20 h-1 (3.5 h doubling t
growth_records.notes: Photoautotrophic cyanobacterium; the limiting resource is light (photons), not a dissolved chemical substrate. Reported mu_max = 0.20 h-1, doubling 3.5 h at 30C, Ii=275 umol photons m-2 s-1. Consistency check: td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.20 = 3.47 h ~ reported 3.5 h. Turbidostat => growth rate equals dilution rate at steady state. Net O2 evolution (surrogate for gross oxygenic photosynthesis) was measured (Clark electrode / oxygraph) and rises linearly with mu, but only plotted in Fig 1B/Fig 2 with no numeric values in text, so O2 rate left null (is_experimental true, value not extractable). CO2 uptake not quantified. Single record captures the reported mu_max condition; the study also spans a continuum of lower mu (~0.05-0.2 h-1) across 4 light regimes with no discrete tabulated rate points.

growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (marine cyanobacterium). Continuous-culture chemostat steady state, so mu=D. Growth rate reported as median 0.193 day-1 (Table 1); converted to per-hour: mu_h=0.193/24=0.00804 /h; doubling_time_h=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.193=3.5914 day x24=86.19 h. dilution_rate_per_h set equal to mu_h (steady state). Dark (CO2) respiration rate 0.0828 day-1 measured (Eq.3, from PC change; Laws et al. 2020). Productivity index (chl-a-normalized photosynthetic C fixation) = 5.13 g C g-1 chl a h-1 (Table 1); not a per-biomass exchange flux so not encoded as uptake/secretion. No substrate uptake or secretion in mmol/gDW/h reported (photoautotroph; DIC and NaNO3 44 uM are the C and N sources). Light 150 umol photons m-2 s-1, 12:12 L:D; salinity 35; TA 2.4 meq/kg.

growth_records.medium: Artificial seawater (Instant Ocean + salts, salinity 35, TA 2.4 meq/kg; Sunda & Hardison 2007)

sec.method: calculated from particulate-carbon decline over scotoperiod, Eq.3 R=2 ln(C12/C0)-D
